acceptance.assertion: Only valid sources whose final event is agent.run.completed or agent.run.blocked, whose configurable grace period elapsed, and whose file identity remains stable may be selected; active started-only and changing sources remain loose.

Compaction requires an intact source/SHA-bound import receipt and exact presence of every source event in the actual Chronik ledger, while receipts remain explicitly nonauthoritative and reconstructible.
Bundle and manifest are immutable, canonical SHA-256-bound, fsynced, fully read back and path-safe before any loose source is removed; partial, orphaned, symlinked or corrupt artifacts fail closed.
An isolated empty-ledger and missing-receipt simulation restores all bundled events and recreates receipts from bundle evidence without source loss or divergent event-id acceptance.
Tests cover publish, directory fsync and unlink failures, source mutation before removal, duplicate and conflicting event IDs across loose and bundled sources, repeated apply and relocatable restore.
Deterministic benchmarks at live-shaped and 10000-source scale remove the eligible loose files, keep at most one full ledger scan per import and remain below the two-minute timer interval.

The exact reviewed diff passes focused and full tests, role and local smoke gates, GitHub CI, merge-bound deployment, live dry-run, controlled apply, repeated import and ledger-integrity readback with the timer restored.

After a source is manifest-bound and removed, the producer and terminalization paths cannot recreate, truncate or rewrite the same logical source path. A later duplicate must be byte-identical or fail closed into an evidence-preserving quarantine with an explicit receipt.

goal: Lose, taskversuchsgebundene Grabowski-Outbox-Quellen nach belegter Terminalität verlustfrei in wenige unveränderliche, hashgebundene Replay-Bundles überführen, ohne aktive Quellen zu verschieben, Receipts zur Autorität zu erheben oder den Chronik-Ledger als operative Wahrheit zu ersetzen.

acceptance_evidence.atomic-replay-bundle: PRs #228 and #229 cover atomic bundle, manifest and reconstructed index publication with fail-closed readback. Live bundle, manifest and archive-index hashes are bound in the compaction proof before 1878 loose sources were removed.
acceptance_evidence.bounded-scale: PR #228 passed live-shaped 2650-source and 10000-source benchmarks well below the two-minute timer interval with one ledger scan and no errors; PR #229 repeated a 10000-source archive-index benchmark successfully.
acceptance_evidence.complete-loss-recovery: PR #228 reports passing empty-ledger plus missing-receipt recovery that restores all bundled events and recreates receipts from bundle evidence.
acceptance_evidence.crash-race-and-rollback-proof: PRs #228, #229, #230 and #232 cover publish/fsync/unlink failures, source races, duplicate/conflicting generations, relocatable restore and the shared lock. The final Chronik chain passed 356 tests at PR #232.
acceptance_evidence.ledger-and-receipt-bound: PR #228 validation and the live apply require intact source/SHA receipts and exact ledger presence. Both post-compaction imports completed without errors; receipts remain explicitly nonauthoritative.
acceptance_evidence.post-bundle-rewrite-guard: The deployed Grabowski guard recomputes event IDs, validates archive evidence and uses the same private lock. Five live cases prove archived suppression, one-time disjoint successor writing after lock release and fail-closed behavior for forged IDs, missing index and invalid lock evidence.
acceptance_evidence.reviewed-release-and-live-proof: The exact Chronik PR chain and Grabowski PR #299 were reviewed, CI-green, merged and deployed. Runtime identities match 73a8df1 and 2a11a714; live dry-run/apply, two imports, ledger hash readback, successful systemd import and timer restoration passed.
acceptance_evidence.terminality-and-grace-bound: PR #228 implements terminality, grace, stable-identity, receipt and ledger eligibility. The live dry-run found 1877 eligible and retained one nonterminal source; the apply compacted 1878 terminal sources after one additional terminal source arrived and retained the remaining nonterminal source.

does_not_establish: future ledger integrity after new writes
automatic bundle deletion or rollup authority
establishes: the Chronik compactor, archive generation model and shared lock are merged and deployed at 73a8df1
the archive-aware Grabowski writer guard is merged and deployed at 2a11a714
the production archive has two manifest generations and 43 source names with two generations
an immediate repeated post-compaction import imported zero events and left the ledger byteidentical
the Chronik import timer was restored enabled and active/waiting after a successful one-shot import

deployment.runtime_integrity: manifest, provenance, runtime binding and module digest valid

does_not_establish: future timer success after the recorded healthy readback
future ledger integrity after new external writes
permission for automatic deletion or bundle rollup

fix: preserve distinct outbox source generations

implementation_draft.historical_note: The original reviewed compactor slice was retained and subsequently hardened for archive indexing, multiple generations and a shared writer/compaction lock.

live_incident_2026_07_18.effect_on_plan: Resolved by the published Chronik generation/index/lock chain and the deployed Grabowski archive-aware writer guard.
live_incident_2026_07_18.pattern: Later loose terminal-only rewrites were strict, shorter event subsets of older complete manifest-bound started/terminal pairs.
live_incident_2026_07_18.symptom: chronik-outbox-import.service failed before new T025 events because loose and bundled variants shared source paths with different bytes.

metadata.outcome: Chronik now compacts only validated terminal loose Grabowski sources into immutable replay bundles under the same private writer/compaction lock used by the deployed producer. The live archive contains two manifest generations and 43 source names with two distinct generations; repeated post-compaction import is idempotent and the timer is restored.
metadata.priority_reason: A real live importer outage demonstrated that post-bundle source recreation is a current integrity defect, not a later-only storage optimization.

chronik_pr_228.live_shaped_benchmark: 2650 sources removed in 3.56 s; repeat import 4.59 s; one ledger scan
chronik_pr_228.scale_benchmark: 10000 sources removed in 13.26 s; repeat import 17.27 s; one ledger scan

validation.chronik_pr_229: 24 focused tests; 348 collected with exit 0; 10000-source benchmark PASS; GitHub CI passed
validation.chronik_pr_230: 47 focused tests; 352 full tests; role contract and diff check PASS
validation.chronik_pr_232: 30 focused compaction tests; 356 full tests; Ruff and diff check PASS; GitHub CI passed

grabowski_pr_299.github_ci: validation on Python 3.10/3.12 and CodeQL Python/Actions passed

verification.source: exact merged PRs plus deployed runtime and live compaction receipts

rollback.procedure: Stop compaction, retain every bundle and remaining loose source, restore the previous immutable Chronik release, reconstruct removed loose files from manifest-bound bundle byte ranges if needed, run isolated ledger replay and integrity verification, and resume the import timer only after a clean readback.
rollback.trigger: Any bundle cannot replay the exact original events, an active or changed source is removed, a partial artifact is accepted, ledger integrity changes unexpectedly, or the importer exceeds the timer safety budget.

conditions: a foreign Chronik worktree, process, task or lease overlaps the selected implementation or live outbox path
the live dry-run reports active, invalid, receiptless, stale-receipt or ledger-unconfirmed sources as eligible
bundle or manifest durability cannot be established before source removal
the empty-ledger recovery simulation or exact event-id/payload comparison fails
the controlled live apply would require deleting a source younger than the approved grace period

title: Chronik-Outbox terminalitätsgebunden kompaktieren
